title: Settings: Triggers
text: Settings: Triggers Choose the triggers you want to be enabled or disabled for debugging, or those that you want to be set as breakpoints. All triggers are enabled by default except the preDisplay trigger. When a trigger is disabled, the Debugger cannot be used to step through its ProcScript statements. Fields The tab displays a list of the Uniface trigger names (abbreviated), starting with application-level triggers, followed by component-level, and so on, down to menu-item level. Within each group of triggers, the abbreviations are sorted alphabetically. For each trigger you can specify one of the following Setting values: Setting Description Enabled Trigger is enabled for debugging. Disabled Trigger cannot be debugged. Break Trigger is set as a breakpoint. Related Links Breakpoints A breakpoint is a marked code line, trigger, error, exception, or condition that forces the application or component to break and the debugger to take control of execution. Settings: I/O Print Choose the level of I/O messages that are displayed in the message frame or transcript window. Settings: Tracing Choose the level or type of ProcScript-related tracing to be displayed in the message frame or transcript window. Settings: Errors Set a specific ProcScript error value ($procerror) as a breakpoint. Settings: Message Frame Specify whether the clrmess ProcScript instruction is enabled or disabled, and whether application messages are sent to the application's message frame or to the application's transcript window.
